warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property calculated: condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack), and dual descriptor for every atom of ferrocene; headline value reported here is the largest f⁺ index.
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical extended tight-binding, no Gaussian basis set, no DFT functional).
• Geometry: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file “ferrocene.xyz”); single-point calculations for the neutral (N), cationic (N–1), and anionic (N+1) charge states – no additional geometry optimisation was performed (i.e. fukui = SP//input geometry).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Total charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1).  The cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states were treated as doublets (multiplicity 2) with charges +1 and –1, respectively (chemkit default for a singlet parent).
• Software/backend: xtb via chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill.
• Convergence & caveats: all three single-point calculations converged; integrity status “ok”, trustworthy = true.  The automatic charge-conservation checks for f⁺ and f⁻ both passed (sums exactly 1.0000) but are flagged with “warning” severity by the engine; they are reproduced verbatim above.

Key results (atom indexing is 1-based, matching the input order):
  – Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺):  Fe (atom 11) f⁺ = 0.22885456
  – Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻):   Fe (atom 11) f⁻ = 0.25938191

The full per-atom Fukui vectors, dual descriptors, and a bar-chart PNG were written to:
  • JSON result  ferrocene_fukui_xtb2.json
  • Plot         …/agent_call_02_plot.png
A complete live engine log was streamed during the run to the standard chemkit “fukui_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out” file in the working directory (path emitted when the job was launched); it can be inspected for SCF details.

The headline numeric value reported above is the electrophilic Fukui index of the iron atom (0.22885456), given to all significant digits provided by the engine.
